data.title: Polar Capital Cancels New Buyback Shares, Updates Voting Rights
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An announcement from Polar Capital Holdings ( (GB:POLR) ) is now available.

Polar Capital Holdings has continued to execute its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 4,383 ordinary shares on 29 May 2026 via Deutsche Bank’s Deutsche Numis arm at prices between 818p and 828p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing the company’s issued ordinary share capital to 100,247,060 and confirming the same number of total voting rights, a figure shareholders can now use as a reference for disclosure thresholds under UK transparency rules.

The latest tranche of buybacks marginally tightens Polar Capital’s share base, which can enhance earnings per share and signal management’s confidence in the company’s valuation. By clarifying the updated voting rights, the group also provides greater certainty for investors and regulatory compliance, underscoring its ongoing capital management discipline amid the current market environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:POLR) stock is a Buy with a £1050.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Polar Capital Holdings stock, see the GB:POLR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on POLR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, POLR is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial stability (low leverage, strong ROE, solid equity base) and attractive valuation (low P/E and high dividend yield). Technicals are supportive with price above key moving averages and positive MACD, though elevated beta and margin compression temper the overall rating.

To see Spark’s full report on POLR stock, click here.

More about Polar Capital Holdings

Polar Capital Holdings is a UK-listed investment management company focused on actively managed funds across a range of asset classes. The group primarily serves institutional and wholesale investors, offering specialist strategies through its listed structure on London’s AIM market.

Average Trading Volume: 366,248

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £771.2M

data.title: Arion Bank Launches Inflation-Linked Covered Bond Auction and Exchange Offer
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An announcement from Arion Banki hf Shs Swedish DR ( (SE:ARION) ) is now available.

Arion Bank plans to issue new ISK-denominated, inflation-linked covered bonds in its ARION CBI 31 series, carrying a fixed 3.65% coupon and maturing in August 2031. The bonds will be sold via a single-price auction on 1 June 2026, with the bank retaining discretion to accept or reject offers in whole or in part.

In parallel, the bank will run an exchange offer allowing holders of the shorter-dated ARION CBI 26 bonds to swap into the new series at a clean price of 100, with settlement expected on 8 June 2026. The move supports Arion Bank’s ongoing covered bond funding programme and offers investors a way to extend duration within the bank’s inflation-linked bond structure while using either cash or existing CBI 26 bonds as payment.

More about Arion Banki hf Shs Swedish DR

Arion Bank hf. is an Icelandic financial institution that provides banking and capital markets services, including funding through covered bond issuance. The bank focuses on the domestic ISK market and uses inflation-linked covered bonds as part of its long-term funding and balance sheet management strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 49,707

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK19.72B

Find detailed analytics on ARION stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

An update from Beazley ( (GB:BEZ) ) is now available.

Zurich Insurance Group has disclosed a 4.006% stake in Beazley plc, equivalent to 24,092,760 voting rights attached to Beazley shares. The threshold crossing occurred on 28 May 2026, with the position held directly through Zurich Insurance Group and Zurich Insurance Company, and no associated financial instruments reported.

The move marks Zurich as a notable shareholder in Beazley, potentially strengthening strategic and capital markets links between the two insurance groups. While the filing is a standard regulatory transparency disclosure, the new holding may be closely watched by investors for signals on sector consolidation, strategic partnerships, or Zurich’s confidence in Beazley’s growth prospects.

data.title: IMI appoints Deutsche Numis to oversee second £250m share buyback tranche
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

IMI plc ( (GB:IMI) ) has issued an announcement.

IMI plc has appointed Deutsche Bank AG’s London branch, trading as Deutsche Numis, to manage the second £250 million tranche of its previously announced £500 million share buyback programme. The second tranche will begin once the initial £250 million phase is completed and is expected to be concluded by 31 December 2026.

The buyback, limited to nearly 10% of IMI’s issued share capital excluding treasury shares, will see Deutsche Numis acquire ordinary shares on a riskless principal basis for subsequent repurchase by the company. The programme is being conducted under existing shareholder authority and in compliance with UK Listing Rules and applicable market abuse regulations, underscoring IMI’s ongoing capital return strategy to shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:IMI) stock is a Buy with a £3080.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IMI plc stock, see the GB:IMI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on IMI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IMI is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (rising margins, strong ROE, and improving leverage), offset by weaker near-term technical momentum and a valuation that is not notably cheap given a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on IMI stock, click here.

More about IMI plc

IMI plc is a UK-based industrial engineering group that designs and manufactures specialist flow control and motion control products for a range of end markets, including energy, transportation, and industrial automation. The company focuses on engineered solutions that improve efficiency, safety, and sustainability for customers worldwide, and its shares are listed on the London Stock Exchange.

Average Trading Volume: 1,572,429

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £6.68B

data.title: Playtech CFO Exercises Share Options and Retains Full Allotment
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest update is out from Playtech ( (GB:PTEC) ).

Playtech has reported a share transaction involving Chief Financial Officer Chris McGinnis, who exercised options over 90,988 ordinary shares under the company’s 2022 Long Term Incentive Plan. The options were nil-cost, and the transaction was conducted outside a trading venue in line with UK Market Abuse Regulation disclosure requirements.

McGinnis plans to cover all tax liabilities arising from the option exercise using his personal funds, meaning he will retain the full allotment of shares rather than selling any portion to meet tax obligations. The move underscores executive alignment with shareholders by increasing the CFO’s direct equity stake in the business while maintaining transparency around senior management dealings in Playtech stock.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PTEC) stock is a Buy with a £460.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Playtech stock, see the GB:PTEC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PTEC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PTEC is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by uneven financial performance and earnings-quality/stability concerns despite generally positive cash generation. Offsetting this, technical signals are supportive with the stock trading above key moving averages and positive momentum indicators, and valuation looks inexpensive based on the very low P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on PTEC stock, click here.

More about Playtech

Playtech plc is a leading global B2B technology provider to the online betting and gaming industry, founded in 1999 and listed on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange. The company offers a proprietary end-to-end turnkey platform, content and services across casino, live casino, sports betting, bingo and poker, operating in more than 50 regulated and regulating jurisdictions with over 7,400 employees in 20 countries.

International Consolidated Airlines ( (GB:IAG) ) has issued an announcement.

International Consolidated Airlines Group disclosed share dealings by senior executives under market abuse regulations, indicating internal equity transactions rather than changes to corporate strategy. Chief Financial and Sustainability Officer Nicholas Cadbury sold 200,000 ordinary shares at £4.072 per share in London on 26 May 2026, while Aer Lingus chair and CEO Lynne Embleton sold 183,000 shares at £4.235 per share on 28 May 2026, signalling notable PDMR activity that investors may monitor for sentiment on the group’s valuation.

The transactions, both classified as initial notifications by persons discharging managerial responsibilities, underscore ongoing alignment of executive holdings with market conditions and regulatory transparency requirements. While the announcement does not indicate any operational shift, such sizable insider sales can influence market perception of the airline group’s prospects and may be scrutinised alongside recent share price performance and sector dynamics.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:IAG) stock is a Buy with a £5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on International Consolidated Airlines stock, see the GB:IAG Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IAG is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (strong margins and cash generation, though leverage and softer 2025 revenue/FCF conversion add risk) and an attractive valuation. These positives are tempered by weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and earnings-call headwinds (FX/fuel volatility, engine constraints, and litigation/cost pressures) despite confident guidance and active shareholder returns.

data.title: Spirax Group NED Constance Baroudel to Become CEO of Spectris Limited While Remaining on Board
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Spirax Group ( (GB:SPX) ) has shared an announcement.

Spirax Group plc has announced that non-executive director Constance Baroudel will leave her role at Halma plc in August 2026 to become chief executive officer of Spectris Limited from 1 September 2026, while retaining her non-executive position on the Spirax Group board. The move underscores Spirax Group’s access to senior industrial leadership and may enhance board-level insight as the company pursues its strategy in net zero-enabling thermal energy and fluid technology solutions across global industrial markets.

Spirax Group continues to leverage its three aligned businesses to provide steam management, industrial electrification, and advanced fluid handling systems for mission-critical applications such as sterilisation, semiconductor manufacturing, and vaccine production. With over 30 manufacturing plants, a workforce of about 10,000, and FTSE 100 status, the group is positioned as a key player in the industrial transition to net zero and in supporting customers’ efficiency and sustainability goals worldwide.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SPX) stock is a Buy with a £8900.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Spirax Group stock, see the GB:SPX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SPX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SPX is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by financial performance: stable revenue and positive cash generation are tempered by multi-year margin/return compression and increased leverage. Technicals add caution due to a negative MACD and mixed moving-average setup, while valuation is constrained by a relatively high P/E despite a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SPX stock, click here.

More about Spirax Group

Spirax Group plc is a UK-headquartered engineering company focused on thermal energy and fluid technology solutions that support industrial efficiency, safety, and decarbonisation. Through its Steam Thermal Solutions, Electric Thermal Solutions, and Watson-Marlow Fluid Technology Solutions businesses, it serves critical sectors from food and healthcare to aviation, semiconductors, and biopharma across nearly 70 countries.

Average Trading Volume: 231,563

Chevron ( (CVX) ) has shared an update.

On May 27, 2026, Chevron announced that R. Hewitt Pate notified its board of his decision to resign as Chief Legal Officer, effective December 31, 2026, in connection with his planned retirement in June 2027. He will continue serving as a non-executive senior advisor until his retirement, helping to support an orderly transition of Chevron’s top legal role and responsibilities, which is significant for continuity in the company’s governance and legal strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (CVX) stock is a Buy with a $230.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chevron stock, see the CVX Stock Forecast page.

CVX scores as moderately attractive: the biggest support is solid underlying financial quality (especially balance-sheet strength) and a constructive earnings-call setup with reiterated guidance, production growth, and disciplined capital returns. The main offsets are weak near-term technical momentum and a demanding P/E multiple for a cyclical business, even with a strong dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on CVX stock, click here.

More about Chevron

Chevron is a global energy corporation engaged primarily in the exploration, production, refining, and marketing of oil and natural gas, as well as the development of lower-carbon energy solutions. The company operates across integrated value chains, supplying fuel, lubricants, and related energy products to industrial, commercial, and consumer markets worldwide.
